data.body:
### [3/10] KEY FINDING — prev_snapshot.json is NOT redundant (CORRECTION of an earlier framing)

An earlier hypothesis "prev_snapshot is redundant, delete it" was PROVEN WRONG by two independent adversarial subagents (red-team hunt-the-breaker + blue-team prove-removable) that CONVERGED, plus direct code reads. DO NOT DELETE prev_snapshot.

WHAT IS TRUE:
- prev_snapshot's DIRECTION-ARBITRATION role IS fully replaced by the per-binding baseline in bindings.json. Proven EMPIRICALLY (not just reasoning): rollout ticket a118-fa8e recorded 30 consecutive divergent=0 shadow passes over ~4h across ~854 bindings + a byte-identical flip pass; d6bd-95c9 hardcoded baseline-consumer-swap always-on; direction test tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_baseline_arbitration.py::test_dryrun_arbitration_pinned_to_baseline feeds a STALE prev_snapshot and asserts the baseline wins. outbound_differ.py:795 -> outbound_fields.py:416-418 overrides arbitration_prev = get_baseline(local_id); the prev_snapshot arg there is a FIXTURE-ONLY fallback = DEAD in prod.
- BUT arbitration is only 1 of ~6 consumers. prev_snapshot is STILL LOAD-BEARING as the EDGE-TRIGGER ANCESTOR: run_differs.py:198 calls differ.compute_mutations(prev_snapshot, curr_snapshot) UNCONDITIONALLY every pass; differ.py:299 "key in jira_state only -> inbound create". Empty prev => every Jira key looks new => re-emits the documented "2050+ inbound_create mutations on every pass" regression (reconcile.py:662-669). The baseline CANNOT cover this — it is keyed by local_id, CONFIRMED-bindings only, with NO representation of UNBOUND native Jira issues.
- Other live prev consumers with no baseline equivalent: dual-identity invariant check_dual_identity_complete (run_differs.py:265, invariants.py:234+) — currently INERT because the fetcher never writes local_id into snapshot entries (differ.py:457-459); edge/level adoption coordination (binding_walk.py:200-218 skip_adopt_keys); corrupt-merge safety canary (_handle_corrupt_snapshot reconcile.py:715-768, also a tickets-branch-partial-merge tripwire); bridge_fsck drift oracle.
- STALE BELIEF corrected: d6bd's keep-reason "needed to advance/seed baselines" is stale — _advance_baselines reads curr not prev (reconcile.py:928); seed_baselines_from_snapshot has ZERO src/ callers (dead code). The REAL reason prev stays alive is the edge-trigger, which d6bd never enumerated.
